showcases.desc: Default lit + shadowed scene (ground + grid + duck).
Full PBR showcase (DamagedHelmet, metallic-roughness).
Data-driven material graph (sphere shaded by showcase.mat.json).
Live runtime material authoring (runtime codegen+dxc compile, no rebuild).
Multi-material scene (three spheres, each a distinct graph material).
Tangent-space normal-mapped material graph (NormalMap node perturbs the shading normal).
Dump a material graph as deterministic JSON (or DOT with --dot): nodes, params, typed ports, edges, resolved PBROutput.
HDR image-based lighting (equirect skybox reflections).
glTF scene-graph import (CesiumMilkTruck node hierarchy).
HDR bloom post-process.
Screen-space ambient occlusion.
Skeletal animation (GPU skinning).
Animation blending between two clips.
Animation state machine (parameter-driven states + cross-fade blend).
Hardware-instanced grid.
Rigid-body physics (impulse solver).
Procedural terrain / heightmap (deterministic NxN displaced grid, lit + shadowed).
Playable game sample (deterministic roll-a-ball collect-the-pickups).
State replication (deterministic snapshot+delta; replica reconstructs the scene).
Simulated network transport + client jitter-buffer interpolation (seeded latency/loss/reorder; smoothing hides the lossy channel).
Client prediction + server reconciliation (local-player prediction over a delayed authoritative sim with a scripted server-only impulse; rewind+replay corrects the misprediction).
Scene/asset streaming (distance-based cell residency + per-frame budget).
Terrain streaming with per-tile LOD (distance-banded tile residency + LOD selection).
Screen-space text / HUD overlay (baked 8x8 font, alpha-blended quads).
Game scene with a live SCORE HUD overlay (own golden; game.png unchanged).
Sorted alpha-blended transparency.
Immediate-mode debug-line visualization.
Capstone scene combining the major features.
Cascaded shadow maps (directional).
Spot light with perspective shadow.
Omnidirectional point-light shadow (cube map).
Clustered / Forward+ many-light shading.

Clustered light culling (Forward+): compute assigns 96 point lights to a 3D cluster grid by hard-radius sphere/AABB; clustered shade == brute-force all-lights byte-identical, 1<maxPerCluster<96, gpu assign total==cpu cluster::AssignLights reference.
Screen-space reflections.
Depth of field (thin-lens circle-of-confusion depth gather; sharp focal plane, blurred fore/background).

Per-object + camera motion blur (velocity-gather post pass; moving content streaks along its screen-space motion vector while static content stays sharp; zero-motion render byte-identical to the un-blurred scene).

Order-independent transparency (Weighted Blended OIT: overlapping transparent layers composited without sorting; the transparent set rendered in two PERMUTED draw orders resolves BYTE-IDENTICALLY, proving order independence).

Parallax occlusion mapping (steep-parallax + binary-refine height-field march in tangent space gives a flat surface per-pixel depth + groove self-shadowing; the heightScale=0 render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the plain normal-mapped render, proving zero UV drift).

Ground-truth ambient occlusion (Jimenez et al. 2016: per-pixel horizon-search over the G-buffer depth field along several slice directions, analytic cosine-weighted visibility integral; ambient term darkened in contacts/concavities; the radius=0 render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-AO scene, proving the integral normalizes to a true identity at zero occlusion).

Subsurface scattering (Jimenez et al. 2015 screen-space separable SSS: a horizontal + a vertical depth-aware diffusion pass blur the lit color of flagged subsurface materials so light bleeds under the surface — the soft translucent glow wrapping the terminator — while non-subsurface pixels pass through; the sssStrength=0 render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the non-SSS lit render, proving the separable kernel collapses to the center tap at zero strength).

Analytic color grading (post-tonemap: the lift/gamma/gain wheels (out = (gain*(c+lift*(1-c)))^(1/gamma)), the ASC-CDL slope/offset/power primary (out = max(c*slope+offset,0)^power), and a luma-preserving saturation control, composed for a cinematic teal-shadows / warm-highlights look on the tonemapped LDR result; fully analytic, no 3D-LUT; the identity-grade render (lift 0, gamma 1, gain 1, slope 1, offset 0, power 1, saturation 1) is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the ungraded tonemap render, proving the grade is a pure pass-through with no rounding/clamp bias at identity).

Contrast-adaptive sharpening (AMD FidelityFX CAS: a fullscreen adaptive 3x3-cross edge sharpen on the tonemapped SDR result — out = (center + w*(up+down+left+right))/(1+4w) with a negative adaptive lobe w driven by the neighborhood luminance min/max (more sharpening in low-contrast regions, less near a hard edge), then clamped per-channel to the neighborhood [min,max] so a sharpen never overshoots past the brightest/darkest neighbor — crisper detail with no ringing halos; the sharpness=0 render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the unsharpened tonemap render, proving the sharpen is a pure pass-through with no bias/clamp drift at zero).

Froxel volumetric fog (a true 3D view-space froxel volume: a compute pass injects per-froxel sun in-scatter + extinction, a second integrates front-to-back into per-froxel in-scatter + transmittance, a fullscreen apply samples it at each pixel's view-depth slice and composites scene*T + in-scatter; the baseDensity=0 render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-fog scene, proving the inject->integrate->apply chain is a pure pass-through at zero density).

DDGI beachhead — probe-grid ray-trace (the first slice of the dynamic diffuse GI arc: a world-space lattice of irradiance probes each trace 16 deterministic Fibonacci-sphere rays against the scene view-space depth field, supplied as a FLAT float SSBO read back from the G-buffer rather than a sampled depth texture, into a flat ray-hit SSBO via a pure compute pass; the GPU ray-hit SSBO is BIT-EXACT to a CPU TraceRayToDepth reference over the same depth field, and a probeCount=0 dispatch leaves the SSBO byte-identical to the cleared upload, proving the ray-trace data layer is deterministic and the disabled path a clean no-op).

Per-froxel clustered-light injection (the CS+CL fusion: the 96 clustered point lights scatter through the froxel fog as colored volumetric shafts — the froxel inject pass maps each froxel to its cluster and ADDS each cluster light's windowed-attenuation * HG-phase * density in-scatter on top of the sun in-scatter; the injectLights=false render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the CS sun-only froxel fog, proving the point-light term is purely additive, and the density=0 render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-fog scene).

Volumetric shadows (sun light shafts through the fog: the froxel inject samples the sun's CSM shadow map per froxel and MULTIPLIES the sun in-scatter by the visibility so the fog has dark shadowed volumes behind geometry + bright foggy sun shafts between them; the volumetricShadows=false render forces visibility to 1 and is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the CV per-froxel clustered-light render, proving the shadow gate is a clean multiplicative identity when off, and the density=0 render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-fog scene).

Screen-space contact shadows (a short per-pixel depth ray-march toward the sun over the G-buffer adds the fine-scale contact occlusion the cascaded shadow map is too coarse to capture — tight dark lines at the bases/creases of small objects in contact — darkening ONLY the direct sun; the maxDist=0 render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-contact-shadow scene, proving the march + sun-apply is a pure pass-through when disabled).

Auto-exposure (histogram eye adaptation: a compute pass builds a luminance histogram of the HDR scene with INTEGER atomics, a reduce pass computes the average log-luminance + the key-value target exposure, and a tonemap variant applies it so a bright scene darkens + a dark scene brightens like the eye; the adaptationEnabled=false render uses the fixed reference exposure E0 and is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the standard fixed-exposure tonemap, proving the histogram->reduce->apply chain is a pure pass-through when disabled).
Water rendering (Gerstner waves, fresnel sky-reflect / scene-refract, sun glint).
Screen-space global illumination (color-bleed indirect diffuse).
SSGI bilateral spatial denoise (edge-preserving smoothing of the indirect-diffuse buffer).
Temporal SSGI accumulation (fixed-N golden-angle-jittered indirect-diffuse averaging).
Screen-space projected decals (top-down decal box on the ground).
Data-driven post-process stack (ordered tonemap/grade/chromatic/vignette/grain chain).
CPU particle / VFX emitter (authorable fountain: spawn rate, lifetime curves, gravity/drag, camera-facing additive billboards).
Volumetric fog / light shafts.
Volumetric clouds (raymarched sunlit cumulus layer in the sky dome).
Cloud shadows on the ground (the cloud field casts dappled shadows on the lit scene by attenuating the direct sun).
Reflection + irradiance probes (local cubemap GI).

Box-projected cubemap reflections (local reflection probe: the specular reflection direction is parallax-corrected to a local box volume — the reflection ray is intersected with the probe box and the cubemap sampled toward the box-exit relative to the probe center — so reflected walls line up with the room geometry instead of appearing infinitely distant; the parallaxStrength=0 render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the standard infinite-cubemap reflection, proving the box-projection is a pure identity at the infinite-box limit).

Runtime cubemap-capture reflection probe (the dynamic counterpart to the box-projected reflection: the scene is RENDERED into the 6 faces of a real captured cubemap from a fixed probe center, then a reflective sphere/floor samples the captured cube — box-projected — so it mirrors the actual surrounding objects rather than a static env; a captured cube face is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the scene rendered directly with that face's view/proj, proving the capture is a faithful scene render).

DDGI probe radiance capture (the second slice of the dynamic diffuse GI arc: for a small world-space grid of irradiance probes, the lit scene is RENDERED into the 6 cube faces from each probe center — looping the single cubemap render target one probe at a time — and read back into a per-probe radiance store, the raw radiance the SH-encode slice will convolve into irradiance; a captured face is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the scene rendered directly with that face's view/proj, and a probeCount=0 grid leaves the radiance store byte-identical to its cleared value, proving the capture is a faithful scene render and the disabled path a clean skip-loop no-op).

DDGI probe SH-encode (the third slice of the dynamic diffuse GI arc: each captured probe cubemap is encoded into 3rd-order real spherical harmonics — 9 coefficients per RGB channel — by a pure compute pass that loops a fixed host-precomputed sample set and accumulates the captured radiance into the SH basis; the sample directions + their SHBasis9 weights + solid-angle weights are host-precomputed and uploaded as exact float32 bits both the GPU encode and the CPU reference read, so the GPU per-probe SH SSBO is BIT-EXACT to a CPU SH-encode over the same read-back radiance, a zero-radiance probe encodes to an all-zero SH, and a probeCount=0 grid leaves the SH SSBO byte-identical to its cleared value — proving the SH-irradiance layer is deterministic and the disabled path a clean dispatch-0 no-op; the per-probe swatches are colored by the SH-reconstructed irradiance).

DDGI probe trilinear SH-interpolation (the fourth slice of the dynamic diffuse GI arc: the per-probe SH irradiance records are trilinearly blended at an arbitrary world position — for a query point, the FLOOR cell whose 8 corner probes bracket it is found and those 8 probes' 3rd-order real-SH coefficients are blended by polynomial trilinear weights — by a pure compute pass, one thread per query point; the NearestProbes 8-corner lookup + the per-corner fma SH accumulation are copied VERBATIM into the shader (mad for the per-corner product) so the GPU blended-SH SSBO is BIT-EXACT to a CPU InterpolateSH reference over the same uploaded probes + query points, a query exactly at a probe position blends to that probe's SH byte-identical (the lattice-point round-trip), and a probeCount=0 grid leaves the output SSBO byte-identical to its cleared value — proving the interpolation layer is deterministic and the disabled path a clean dispatch-0 no-op; the golden renders a dense grid of swatch spheres colored by the SH-reconstructed irradiance, a smooth interpolated color field between the probes).

DDGI per-probe distance-moment capture (the first slice of the DDGI VISIBILITY sub-arc — the data layer that makes the Chebyshev occlusion test possible, killing DDGI light-leak through walls: for the same small world-space grid of irradiance probes, each probe renders the scene GEOMETRY from its centre into the 6 cube faces — looping the single cubemap render target one probe at a time — writing the linear WORLD-DISTANCE d = length(worldPos - probeCentre) packed as the two moments float2(d, d*d) instead of radiance, then reads the faces back into a per-probe distance-moment store; the second moment d*d is a BARE multiply so, GIVEN the same read-back distance bytes, the GPU-written moment float2(d, d*d) is BIT-EXACT to the CPU MomentsFromDistance(d) reference (the GPU==CPU proof is on the moment-from-distance step — the sqrt-derived distance itself is render-equivalence per backend, not claimed bit-identical), a captured distance face is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the scene rendered directly with that face's view/proj through the same distance shader, and a probeCount=0 grid leaves the moment store byte-identical to its cleared value — proving the visibility capture is a faithful geometric render and the disabled path a clean skip-loop no-op; produces NO visible lighting change yet (the next slice consumes it for Chebyshev occlusion weighting); debug-viz = per-probe swatch spheres coloured by the probe's mean captured distance, near warm / far cool, over the colored room).

DDGI global illumination (the fifth and final slice of the dynamic diffuse GI arc — the VISIBLE payoff: a single-bounce indirect-diffuse term is added to a sibling lit-pass variant so a Cornell box shows colored indirect light bleed — the red wall bleeds reddish onto the floor, the green wall bleeds green — ≈ UE4-parity local GI; per pixel the 8 nearest irradiance probes are trilinearly blended into a single 3rd-order real-SH irradiance record (NearestProbes + InterpolateSH + SHEvaluate copied VERBATIM into the shader, mad for every accumulation) reconstructed toward the surface normal and added as indirect*albedo*(1-metallic)*giStrength; the ProbeSH[] rides in a fragment-stage storage buffer bound via the existing cluster path (no new RHI); the indirect term is added LAST so giStrength=0 (or a probeCount=0 grid) makes it a literal +0.0 and the render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-GI baseline through the same pipeline — proving the disabled path a clean no-op that leaves all the existing lit goldens untouched, while the enabled frame shows coherent recognizable Cornell color bleed).

DDGI multi-bounce / 2nd light bounce (the eighth slice of the dynamic diffuse GI arc — the next GI-quality leap: single-bounce DDGI (the DH–DN arc) captures only DIRECT light into the probes so indirect light bounces exactly ONCE, whereas multi-bounce feeds the 1st-bounce GI BACK into a second probe capture so a second capture sees the indirect light on the surfaces and the composite gets a brighter, more filled-in 2nd bounce — the step from UE4-style DDGI toward Lumen's infinite-bounce; done as a FIXED 2-iteration pass in one frame (deterministic, golden-able), not a temporal feedback loop: bounce 0 = the existing direct-light capture -> SH0, bounce 1 (only when bounceCount>=2) re-captures each probe with a NEW capture-with-GI fragment (a sibling of the direct-capture shader that adds the DN single-bounce indirect term — InterpolateIrradianceSH(wpos,N)*(1-metallic)*albedo*giStrength, sampling SH0) -> SH-encode -> SH1, and the composite binds SH(bounceCount-1); two SH[] storage buffers ride the EXISTING cluster path (no new RHI); INTERNALLY the bounceCount=1 render SKIPS the bounce-1 capture so it is BYTE-IDENTICAL (SHA) to the DN single-bounce render — the make-safe no-op — while the bounceCount=2 frame is measurably BRIGHTER (the bounced wall light itself bounces), the bounce-1 SH1 SSBO is BIT-EXACT to a CPU SH-encode of the read-back bounce-1 radiance, and two runs are byte-identical; golden = the brighter 2nd-bounce frame; the direct-capture shader + the DN lit_ddgi shader + their goldens untouched via sibling-shader isolation).

DDGI Chebyshev occlusion weighting (the second slice of the DDGI VISIBILITY sub-arc — the VISIBLE leak-fix: a sibling lit-pass variant weights each of the 8 nearest irradiance probes' SH contribution by a per-probe Chebyshev (variance-shadow-style) VISIBILITY weight read from the per-probe distance-moment cube, so a surface NO LONGER receives indirect light from probes geometrically OCCLUDED from it — a probe behind a wall stops bleeding light through it, the #1 quality gap of plain irradiance-probe GI and the real DDGI (Majercik et al. 2019) fix; for each corner probe the probe->point direction + linear distance index the distance cube (mean=m[0], variance=m[1]-mean²), cheb = dist<=mean ? 1 : variance/(variance+(dist-mean)²) is the Chebyshev upper bound in [0,1], vis = lerp(1, cheb, occlusionStrength), and the per-probe trilinear weight is scaled by vis, renormalised, and the SH blended with mad; the distance moments ride a second fragment-stage storage buffer at the next free cluster-set slot via the existing bind path (no new RHI); INTERNALLY the occlusionStrength=0 render BRANCHES to the VERBATIM DN composite path so it is BYTE-IDENTICAL (SHA) to the DN lit_ddgi render — the make-safe no-op — while the occlusionStrength=1 frame shows the leak through the occluder GONE/attenuated and differs from frame A, the visibility-math CPU mirror matches the closed form, and two runs are byte-identical; golden = the leak-fixed frame, the occluder no longer bleeds; the DN lit_ddgi shader + its goldens untouched via sibling-shader isolation).

Planar reflections (flat mirror-plane scene reflection: the scene is RENDERED a second time through the camera REFLECTED across the mirror plane — householder reflection + Lengyel oblique near-clip at the mirror plane + flipped front-face winding — into a 2D reflection target, then the mirror floor samples that target at its own screen-space position and blends lerp(matte, reflection, reflectivity); the reflectivity=0 render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the matte non-reflective render, proving the reflection blend is a pure pass-through when off).
Temporal anti-aliasing (jittered accumulation).
Frustum culling visualization (overview camera + kept/culled bounds).

Virtual-geometry meshlet/cluster decomposition (the BEACHHEAD of the Nanite-style virtual-geometry arc: a sphere's index buffer is partitioned by a pure-CPU integer-deterministic Morton-sort into clusters of up to 128 triangles, each with a conservative per-cluster AABB + bounding sphere; the reordered index buffer is uploaded once and each cluster drawn as an index sub-range with its deterministic per-cluster hash color, so the sphere shows as coherent flat-colored SPATIAL cluster patches — the cluster data structure the whole DT/DU/DV arc builds on; the partition is COMPLETE (Sum(triCount)==T, every triangle covered exactly once) and two builds are BYTE-IDENTICAL; no new RHI (existing draw + push-constant surface), no GPU math (the color is a pure CPU integer hash)).
GPU-driven culling + indirect draw (compute compaction, GPU draw count).
GPU multi-draw-indirect batching (144 objects in 1 draw; MDI==per-draw byte-identical).
Bindless textures (multi-texture scene via 1 descriptor array; bindless==bound byte-identical).
Fully-GPU-driven pass (100-object multi-material scene via 1 MDI call + 1 bindless bind; gpu-driven==bound byte-identical).
Fully-GPU-driven-culled pass (compute frustum-cull -> compacted MDI + bindless; GPU decides AND draws; gpu-culled==CPU-culled byte-identical, gpu drawn==cpuRef).

Virtual-geometry per-CLUSTER frustum cull -> indirect cluster draw (Slice DT: an instance grid of DS-clustered spheres is decomposed into (instance x cluster) records each with a per-cluster world bounding sphere; a compute shader frustum-culls + ORDER-compacts the survivors into the MDI command buffer + the compacted per-draw SSBO + writes the survivor count, then ONE DrawIndexedMultiIndirect renders exactly the in-frustum cluster-instances; the per-cluster analogue of the per-OBJECT GPU cull — GPU draw-count == CPU SurvivorClusterCount frustum.h reference EXACT, GPU-culled image == CPU-CullClusterInstances image byte-identical, all-in-frustum == full unculled draw byte-identical, two-run deterministic; no new RHI).

Hi-Z occlusion culling (depth pre-pass -> Hi-Z max-depth pyramid -> compute frustum+occlusion cull; objects fully hidden behind a near occluder are dropped; occlusion-culled==frustum-only byte-identical, occluded>0, gpu occluded==cpu hiz reference).

Virtual-geometry per-CLUSTER Hi-Z occlusion cull -> indirect cluster draw (Slice DU: DT's per-cluster frustum cull PLUS CJ's Hi-Z occlusion test at cluster granularity; an occluder wall in front of a back row of DS-clustered spheres + non-occluded spheres; a depth pre-pass -> Hi-Z max-depth pyramid -> a compute shader frustum-culls THEN drops any cluster-instance whose bounding-sphere AABB is fully hidden behind the occluder, ORDER-compacts the survivors into the MDI buffer + writes the survivor count, then ONE DrawIndexedMultiIndirect renders exactly the visible cluster-instances; conservative -> occlusion-culled==frustum-only byte-identical, occlusion-disabled==DT byte-identical, survivor count DROPS, GPU count==CPU SurvivorClusterCountHiZ + GPU-culled==CPU-CullClusterInstancesHiZ byte-identical, two-run deterministic; no new RHI).

Virtual-geometry discrete cluster-LOD selection by projected screen-space error (Slice DV, the FINAL slice of the virtual-geometry CORE: a row of instances at INCREASING distance each picks one of 3 pre-baked discrete LOD tessellations — each DS-cluster-decomposed — by its projected screen-space error; near instances draw LOD0 (full detail, many fine clusters), far instances draw a coarser LOD (fewer coarse clusters) — a visible detail falloff; a compute shader computes the squared view distance + SelectLod (a host-precomputed projScale + std::fma + squared-distance comparison, NO sqrt -> the selected-LOD integer is bit-exact CPU==GPU) and ORDER-compacts the selected LOD's clusters into the MDI command buffer + the compacted per-draw SSBO + writes the per-instance selected-LOD int + the total command count, then ONE DrawIndexedMultiIndirect renders the selected-LOD clusters; the Nanite LOD primitive kept deterministic via pre-baked LODs (no runtime simplifier) — GPU per-instance selected-LOD == CPU SelectLod BIT-EXACT (memcmp), forceLod0/errorScale=0 == the full-detail render BYTE-IDENTICAL (the disabled path), the selected set spans >=2 distinct LODs, distance-monotonic (farther never finer), two-run deterministic; no new RHI).

Virtual-geometry VISIBILITY BUFFER (Slice DW, the Nanite-style rendering beachhead: the same DT survivor cluster MDI draw, but the fragment output is a SINGLE uint32 (clusterID<<7 | SV_PrimitiveID) into an R32_Uint render target — the (clusterID, triangleID) visibility buffer that decouples geometry rasterization from material shading; NO lighting/resolve; the integer RT is ReadRenderTarget'd back into uint32[w*h] and proven bit-exact — visbuffer self-consistent + coverage == CPU CullClusterInstances survivor set EXACT, two renders BYTE-IDENTICAL, GPU==CPU interior coverage EXACT; the image golden is a CPU-coloring of the read-back IDs (bg->clear, else hashColor(clusterID)); the only new RHI is the additive Format::R32_Uint — existing goldens untouched).

Virtual-geometry DEFERRED MATERIAL RESOLVE (Slice DX, the 2nd visibility-buffer slice: a fullscreen deferred pass texel-fetches the DW R32_Uint vis-buffer per pixel, unpacks (clusterID,triID), looks up the covering triangle's 3 vertices via the cluster-meta/index/vertex SSBOs, computes a FLAT geometric normal (normalize(cross(p1-p0,p2-p0)), std::fma) + Lambert shade, and outputs the lit image — Nanite's decoupled geometry->material architecture where shading cost is per-pixel-visible; the spheres render FLAT-SHADED/faceted lit via the vis-buffer instead of a forward pass; PROVEN: ID-provenance bit-exact at interior pixels (the resolve reads the DW/CPU-predicted covering cluster), GPU==CPU resolve-MATH bit-exact at those interior pixels (ResolveFlatShade CPU mirror == the GPU shaded RGB memcmp), two-run determinism, and a resolve-vs-forward SMOKE bound (< eps, explicitly NOT byte-identical — cross-vendor rasterizer divergence makes resolve==forward infeasible); the image golden is the resolved lit image; reuses the existing BindTexture (integer vis-buffer as a sampled uint texture, texel-fetched no sampler) + BindLightClusters (the 3 fragment-stage SSBOs) + the fullscreen-pass path — no new RHI symbol).

Nanite SOFTWARE-RASTER (Slice SW1, the BEACHHEAD of the software-rasterization flagship: a PURE-CPU deterministic integer/fixed-point software rasterizer scan-converts a small fixed set of cluster triangles into a packed depth|id visibility buffer — the SAME (clusterID<<7 | triID) identity as DW's hardware vis-buffer, but rasterized on the CPU with integer edge functions — front-most surface per pixel via a serial min over a 32-bit depth|id key (depth in the HIGH bits so smaller=nearer wins; the CPU mirror of the GPU-compute SW2's InterlockedMin). The ONE floating-point step (ProjectToScreenVert: MulPointDivide->NDC->pixel with the Vulkan Y-flip->round to 1/16 sub-pixel fixed-point) is host-only; the coverage test is pure integer edge functions E(p,q,r) with int64 intermediates evaluated at each pixel CENTER under a TOP-LEFT fill rule (a shared edge covers each boundary pixel EXACTLY once — watertight, no double-cover/gap). PROOFS (fail loudly): (1) bit-exact reference (a hash of packed[]) + {w,h,coveredPixels,tris}; (2) shuffled-order rasterization BYTE-IDENTICAL (the min is order-independent); (3) a sub-pixel triangle COVERED (a hardware sample-at-center would miss it — the raison d'etre of the software raster); (4) an empty triangle set == the cleared buffer BYTE-IDENTICAL. The image golden is a CPU-coloring of the integer vis-buffer (bg->clear, else hashColor(visId>>7)) — identical both backends by construction. PURE CPU, NO GPU, NO new RHI (a clean present); the GPU-compute SW2 will copy this math verbatim + prove bit-identical).

Nanite SOFTWARE-RASTER GPU (Slice SW2, the GPU COMPUTE software rasterizer — the 2nd software-rasterization slice after SW1's CPU reference: shaders/swraster.comp scan-converts a clustered mesh's triangles (SphereGeometry -> BuildMeshlets, several hundred tris, plus a sub-pixel triangle) into a w*h depth|id visibility-buffer SSBO (a RWStructuredBuffer<uint>) via InterlockedMin — ONE thread per cluster-triangle running the int64 integer edge functions + the top-left fill rule + the flat min-depth copied VERBATIM from render/swraster.h over the HOST-SNAPPED integer ScreenVerts (the GPU does ZERO floating point — it consumes the same integer ScreenVerts the host snapped via ProjectToScreenVert). The commutative InterlockedMin (the GPU mirror of SW1's serial std::min) makes the result order-independent, so integer coverage + integer depth + a commutative atomic => the GPU vis-buffer is provably == the CPU swraster.h::RasterClusters reference at EVERY pixel, full-frame (not interior-only — the integer edge test is exact at edges too, unlike a hardware rasterizer), on any vendor (Vulkan DXC / Metal spirv-cross). PROOFS (fail loudly): (1) GPU==CPU BIT-IDENTICAL full-frame (memcmp(gpuVis, cpuVis.packed)==0 — the make-or-break); (2) two dispatches BYTE-IDENTICAL (atomic-min order-independence); (3) a sub-pixel triangle COVERED (a hardware sample-at-center would miss it); (4) triCount=0 -> gVis all-kSwClear (the disabled-path no-op). The image golden is a CPU-coloring of the read-back integer vis-buffer (bg->clear, else hashColor(visId>>kTriIdBits)) — identical both backends by construction. NO new RHI (RWStructuredBuffer<uint> with BufferUsage::Storage + InterlockedMin — the existing InterlockedAdd family — + BindStorageBuffer/DispatchCompute/ReadBuffer/ComputeToFragmentBarrier)).

Nanite SOFTWARE-RASTER vis-buffer -> DEFERRED MATERIAL RESOLVE (Slice SW4, the back-half of the software-rasterization arc: drive the EXISTING visresolve.frag deferred material resolve from the SOFTWARE visibility buffer, completing a full software-raster -> deferred-shade pipeline. shaders/swraster.comp fills the SW depth|id visibility-buffer SSBO over the SW2 clustered-sphere scene (SphereGeometry(24,16) -> BuildMeshlets, ~769 tris, 512x512), then a tiny fullscreen 'unpack' blit (shaders/swraster_resolve_blit.frag) converts that SSBO into the R32_Uint visId render target the resolve already reads: per pixel idx=y*width+x, out=(packed==kSwClear)?kVisBackground:(packed&0xFFFF) — strip the HIGH-16 depth, keep the LOW-16 visId=(clusterID<<7)|triID (kSwClear==kVisBackground==0xFFFFFFFF so the sky sentinel passes through). The UNCHANGED visresolve.frag (reused BYTE-FOR-BYTE) then texel-fetches the visId RT + the cluster-meta/index/vertex SSBOs and flat-shades it into a Lambert-lit image; the ClusterMeta is built from the SW meshlets directly (clusterID IS the meshlet index, triOffset = meshlets[cl].triOffset, identity model). PROOFS (fail loudly): (1) blit GPU==CPU unpack BIT-IDENTICAL full-frame (every visId RT texel == the CPU unpack of the SW SSBO); (2) SW resolve == CPU vg::ResolvePixel expectation @interior (a direct SW==HW byte-exact image compare is NOT cross-rasterizer-feasible — the SW flat-min-depth vs a HW interpolated-depth pick different intra-cluster triangle winners, the documented visresolve.h coverage caveat); (3) GPU==CPU shade @interior EXACT (vg::ResolveFlatShade + EncodeBGRA8 memcmp vs the GPU resolved BGRA); (4) two renders BYTE-IDENTICAL; (5) disabled-path all-sky no-op (0 raster groups -> SSBO all kSwClear -> blit all kVisBackground -> uniform sky); (6) a coherent SHADED mesh (shaded>0, not uniform). The image golden is the Lambert-lit deferred-resolved software-rasterized sphere. NO new RHI: the blit's SW SSBO rides the existing BindLightClusters fragment-storage path (set 3 binding 13) + a {width,height} UBO, the resolve rides BindTexture (the R32_Uint RT) + BindLightClusters (the cluster SSBOs) + ComputeToFragmentBarrier; the blit frag is plain MSL (writes an integer SV_Target — NO --msl-version 20200, unlike visresolve.frag's integer texture.read). On Metal swraster.comp is int64/Vulkan-only, so the CPU render/swraster.h::RasterClusters reference fills the SSBO -> the SAME blit + resolve -> identical by construction).

Virtual Shadow Maps PAGE-NEEDED MARKING (Slice VA, the Virtual Shadow Maps flagship beachhead: build the virtual directional-CLIPMAP page table + mark which virtual shadow pages the visible receivers need, as a pure INTEGER compute pass — NO rendering, NO new RHI; a fixed clipmap (levels=4, vpps=8, pageSize=128, level0WorldExtent=16) + a fixed ground-grid receiver point-set feed shaders/vsm_mark.comp: one thread per receiver runs SelectClipmapLevel (an INTEGER threshold-ladder against host-precomputed level0WorldExtent*2^L thresholds — NO transcendental log2, the GPU==CPU determinism crux) + MarkPage (project into the level's top-down ortho, subtract/divide/floor) -> resident[pageId]=1 (an order-independent integer SET); ReadBuffer reads the resident[] set and it is proven BIT-EXACT vs the CPU vsm::MarkResidentPages reference (memcmp, NO FP tolerance), markingEnabled=false -> empty set, two runs byte-identical; the image golden is a top-down concentric-clipmap debug-viz CPU-colored from the read-back integer set (resident page -> hashColor(pageId) over its clipmap level, else dark) -> identical both backends by construction; reuses BufferUsage::Storage + DispatchCompute + ReadBuffer — no new RHI symbol).

Virtual Shadow Maps PHYSICAL-PAGE DEPTH RENDERING (Slice VB, the 2nd Virtual Shadow Maps slice: allocate PHYSICAL atlas tiles to VA's resident virtual pages — a deterministic integer virtual->physical indirection table (vsm::AllocatePhysicalPages walks the resident set in ascending pageId order, each resident page -> the next sequential physical tile, non-resident/overflow -> kNoTile; priority = ascending pageId == finest/nearest clipmap level first) — then RENDER each resident page's shadow-casters' depth into its tile via the CSM SetViewport atlas loop, each with that page's clipmap-level ortho (vsm::PageWorldOrtho); a fixed caster grid (boxes+spheres) + the fixed clipmap (levels=4, vpps=8, level0WorldExtent=16) -> MarkResidentPages -> AllocatePhysicalPages -> a 16x16-tile 2048² physical atlas (CreateRenderTarget at atlas size). PROOFS: the indirection table is two-run byte-identical AND == the CPU AllocatePhysicalPages reference (memcmp), {residentPages, physicalTiles, atlas WxH}, the colorized depth atlas two-run byte-identical, markingEnabled=false -> 0 resident -> 0 allocated -> empty (cleared) atlas; the golden is the colorized depth atlas — vsm_depth.frag writes SV_Position.z -> grayscale (near=bright/far=dark), ReadRenderTarget'd from the COLOR atlas, non-resident tiles the fixed clear color, resident tiles per-page caster-depth silhouettes. Reuses the CSM shadow-atlas RHI (CreateRenderTarget + SetViewport + ReadRenderTarget) + the pure-CPU integer allocator — no new RHI symbol).

Virtual Shadow Maps LIT-PASS INDIRECTION SAMPLE (Slice VC, the 3rd Virtual Shadow Maps slice: the LIT PASS samples the VSM -> a SHADOWED scene. Per receiver pixel the sibling lit_vsm.frag (a copy of lit_csm.frag) picks the clipmap level via the VA integer threshold-ladder SelectClipmapLevel (copied VERBATIM from render/vsm.h — NO transcendental, bit-exact GPU==CPU), projects wpos into the level's top-down clipmap page via the VERBATIM MarkPage projection -> pageId = PageId(level,px,py), looks up the physical tile in the virtual->physical indirection table SSBO (gIndirection[pageId], [[vk::binding(13,3)]], bound via the EXISTING usesLightClusters/BindLightClusters path — no new RHI), and if the page is resident projects wpos into THAT page's PageWorldOrtho light-space and samples the physical tile in the VSM DEPTH atlas (bound via SetShadowMap) with tile-clamped 3x3 PCF (SampleCascadePCF adapted: the tile origin from PhysicalTileOrigin, no neighbour bleed) -> a VSM-shadowed image; non-resident page (tile==kNoTile) -> shadow=1.0 (lit). THE make-or-break NO-OP: shadow = lerp(1.0, shadow, vsmEnabled) -> vsmEnabled=0 yields shadow==1.0 EXACTLY -> the render is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the unshadowed lit. PROOFS: (1) vsmEnabled=0 == unshadowed BYTE-IDENTICAL (the make-or-break no-op); (2) frame B (vsmEnabled=1) != A (shadows active); (3) page-lookup GPU==CPU bit-exact at interior receiver pixels (the verbatim integer chain); (4) two-run determinism; (5) VSM vs CSM (1-level) smoke (< eps, NOT diff-0 — the atlas layouts differ); the golden is frame B, the VSM-shadowed scene. Reuses BindLightClusters (indirection) + SetShadowMap (atlas) + the FrameData UBO (params) + the lit-pass pipeline; lit.frag/lit_csm.frag + their goldens untouched (sibling isolation) — no new RHI symbol).

Virtual Shadow Maps PER-PAGE CACHING (Slice VD, the FINAL Virtual Shadow Maps slice: skip re-rendering physical pages whose content is unchanged — UE5's VSM key performance property. A per-page CONTENT KEY (vsm::PageContentKey, a fixed FNV-1a 64-bit hash of the page's clipmap level + its world region (PageWorldOrtho, quantized) + the casters whose bounds overlap that region (PageOverlapsCaster; each overlapping caster's meshId + quantized model-matrix xformHash, ascending order) -> integer/bit-exact, transcendental-free, two identical scenes -> identical keys) + a per-page CACHE (vsm::VsmPageCache{key,valid}; PageCacheHit = valid && key==newKey, PageCacheUpdate records it). A page re-renders iff !PageCacheHit; a HIT SKIPS the render and keeps the existing (already-correct) tile depth -> BYTE-IDENTICAL. The showcase: Pass 1 populate (cache empty -> all miss) -> atlasA; Pass 2 same scene (every page HIT, 0 re-renders) -> atlasB; Pass 3 move ONE caster -> only its overlapping page(s) re-render, the rest cached -> atlasC, plus the moved scene rendered fully-uncached -> atlasC_full. PROOFS (fail loudly): (1) atlasB==atlasA BYTE-IDENTICAL AND hits==residentPages (all-cached==fresh — the make-or-break); (2) atlasC==atlasC_full BYTE-IDENTICAL AND misses==the small affected-page count (the cache is transparent + the invalidation minimal); (3) two-run determinism; prints {residentPages,hits,misses}. The golden is a cache-status viz: the Pass-3 depth atlas with resident tiles CPU-tinted by cache state (CACHED=green, RE-RENDERED=red) from the integer cache state -> identical both backends. PURE host-side cache over VB's existing physical-page render + readback — "skip a page render" = not issuing that page's SetViewport+draw; reuses CreateRenderTarget + SetViewport + ReadRenderTarget — NO new RHI symbol).

Runtime Virtual Texturing PAGE-NEEDED FEEDBACK MARKING (Slice VT1, the BEACHHEAD of the Runtime Virtual Texturing flagship — UE5's literal RVT: a virtual texture far larger than VRAM, sampled through a virtual->physical page indirection driven by per-pixel FEEDBACK. Build the virtual mip-pyramid page table + mark which virtual texture pages a set of sample-requests need, as a pure INTEGER compute pass — NO rendering, NO new RHI; the STRUCTURAL TWIN of the VSM Slice VA marking pass, applied to TEXTURE pages. A fixed virtual texture (mipLevels=4, pageSize=128, virtualPagesPerSideMip0=16 -> a 2048²-mip0 mip pyramid, 340 pages over 4 mips; pagesPerSide(mip)=max(1,vpps0>>mip), mipPageOffset = the PREFIX-SUM of pagesPerSide(k)² — NOT mip*vpps², the VT-vs-VSM crux) + a fixed deterministic (UV,mip) sample-request set: the host HOST-SNAPS each request to its integer (mip,px,py) page coords (vt::SnapRequest runs the UV->page quantization floor(u*pagesPerSide(mip)) CPU-side so the GPU does ZERO floating point — the swraster.h host-snap discipline), and shaders/vt_feedback.comp (one thread per request) computes pageId = mipPageOffset(mip)+py*pagesPerSide(mip)+px (PageId copied VERBATIM from render/vt.h) -> feedback[pageId]=1 (an order-independent integer SET); ReadBuffer reads the feedback[] set and it is proven BIT-EXACT vs the CPU vt::MarkFeedbackPages reference (memcmp, NO FP tolerance), feedbackEnabled=false -> empty set, two dispatches byte-identical; the image golden is a per-mip page-grid debug-viz CPU-colored from the read-back integer set (resident page -> hashColor(pageId), else dark; the mips laid out in a row, mip 0 widest) -> identical both backends by construction; reuses BufferUsage::Storage + DispatchCompute + ReadBuffer — no new RHI symbol).

Runtime Virtual Texturing PHYSICAL TILE-POOL ALLOCATION + virtual->physical INDIRECTION TABLE (Slice VT2, the 2nd Runtime Virtual Texturing slice after VT1's page-needed feedback: given VT1's resident page SET, ALLOCATE physical tiles in a finite tile pool and build the virtual->physical INDIRECTION table the future sampling pass reads — the DIRECT analog of VSM Slice VB's AllocatePhysicalPages, applied to TEXTURE pages. A deterministic ascending-priority integer allocator: vt::AllocatePhysicalTiles walks pageId 0..pageCount-1 ASCENDING and assigns each resident page (feedback[pageId]==1) the next sequential physical tile while nextTile < pool.tileCapacity(), else kNoTile (non-resident OR overflow); ascending pageId == mip-major == finest-mip-first priority (the nearest/most-detailed pages win the pool when it overflows). Reuses VT1's scene (mipLevels=4, pageSize=128, virtualPagesPerSideMip0=16 -> 340 pages) + the SAME 576 (UV,mip) requests -> host vt::MarkFeedbackPages builds the resident set -> a SINGLE-THREAD GPU allocator (shaders/vt_alloc.comp, [numthreads(1,1,1)]: one thread walks pageId ascending maintaining nextTile, writing gIndirection[pageId] = (resident && nextTile<cap) ? nextTile++ : 0xFFFFFFFF) over a finite VtTilePool{tilesPerSide=12} (144 tiles < the resident set -> the overflow/kNoTile path is exercised); allocation is INHERENTLY sequential (nextTile depends on prior assignments) so the GPU mirrors the CPU with a deterministic serial scan. ReadBuffer reads the indirection table and it is proven BIT-EXACT vs the CPU vt::AllocatePhysicalTiles reference (memcmp, NO FP tolerance — the GPU allocator reproduces the CPU table); allocated == min(resident,capacity), every allocated tile index unique + in [0,capacity), ascending-priority (the first capacity resident pages by pageId are the allocated ones), every overflow resident page kNoTile; allocEnabled=false -> all kNoTile (the disabled no-op); two dispatches byte-identical. The image golden is a per-mip page-grid debug-viz CPU-colored from the read-back integer indirection (allocated page -> hashColor(tileIndex), resident-but-overflow -> a distinct dim color, non-resident -> dark) -> identical both backends by construction; reuses BufferUsage::Storage + DispatchCompute + ReadBuffer — no new RHI symbol).

Runtime Virtual Texturing procedural PAGE GENERATION into the PHYSICAL ATLAS (Slice VT3, the 3rd Runtime Virtual Texturing slice after VT1's feedback + VT2's allocation: GENERATE each allocated virtual page's deterministic procedural CONTENT into its physical tile in the atlas — the analog of VSM Slice VB's physical-page depth render, writing procedural texture COLOR instead of caster depth. Kept PURE INTEGER so the atlas is cross-backend bit-identical: the physical atlas is a flat RWStructuredBuffer<uint> of atlasW*atlasH RGBA8-packed texels, and each texel's color is a pure integer function PageTexel(pageId,mip,localXY) — a per-page base color from an FNV/avalanche hash of (pageId,mip) MODULATED by an 8×8 integer checkerboard + a coarse gradient over the page-local (lx,ly), every channel floored into [0x40,0xFF] so no generated texel collides with kAtlasClear (0xFF101010). Reuses VT1/VT2 (VtTexture mipLevels=4/pageSize=128/vpps0=16 -> 340 pages + the SAME 576 (UV,mip) requests -> host vt::MarkFeedbackPages -> vt::AllocatePhysicalTiles) over a finite VtTilePool{tilesPerSide=16} (256 physical tiles > the resident set of 212, so 212 tiles are allocated and 44 stay free/dark), builds the host reverse table tilePageId[tileIndex]=pageId (vt::BuildTilePageId, the inverse of the indirection over the allocated set), and dispatches shaders/vt_pagegen.comp — ONE thread per atlas texel (a race-free parallel MAP, NO sequential dependency, NO atomics, unlike VT2's serial allocator) — over the 1024×1024 (16 tiles × 64-px pages) atlas SSBO: each texel finds its tile, reads tilePageId to recover the owning pageId, UnpackPageId's it -> (mip,px,py) (verbatim render/vt.h), and writes PageTexel; unallocated tiles -> kAtlasClear; genEnabled=0 -> all kAtlasClear. ReadBuffer reads the RGBA8-packed atlas and it is proven BIT-EXACT vs the CPU vt::GeneratePhysicalAtlas reference (memcmp, NO FP tolerance — the GPU per-texel generator reproduces the CPU atlas); exactly the allocated tiles are non-clear with every allocated tile fully written (no kAtlasClear hole) and every unallocated region all kAtlasClear; genEnabled=false -> all kAtlasClear (the disabled no-op); two dispatches byte-identical. The image golden is the atlas decoded directly from the RGBA8-packed uints (each allocated tile a tinted patterned page, unallocated dark) -> identical both backends by construction; reuses BufferUsage::Storage + DispatchCompute + ReadBuffer — no new RHI symbol).

Runtime Virtual Texturing material-pass SAMPLE through the INDIRECTION (Slice VT4, the 4th Runtime Virtual Texturing slice after VT1's feedback + VT2's allocation + VT3's page-gen: SAMPLE the virtual texture through the virtual->physical INDIRECTION — the round-trip that makes RVT a usable texture. A compute pass reconstructs the virtual texture's mip-0 image in VIRTUAL UV space by, per virtual texel, computing its pageId -> looking up indirection[pageId] -> offsetting into the physical atlas -> reading the texel (NEAREST — a direct integer texel read, NO filtering). Kept INTEGER nearest-sample (NOT bilinear) so the sample path is cross-backend BIT-IDENTICAL (the zero-differing-pixel posture of VT1-VT3); bilinear filtering + perspective-mapped geometry are an explicit DEFERRED float refinement (VT5+). The miss color kVtMiss (0xFFFF00FF magenta, distinct from kAtlasClear + any PageTexel) marks a non-resident page. Reuses VT3's full pipeline (the SAME VtTexture mipLevels=4/pageSize=128/vpps0=16 + 576 (UV,mip) requests -> host vt::MarkFeedbackPages -> vt::AllocatePhysicalTiles over VtTilePool{tilesPerSide=16} -> vt::GeneratePhysicalAtlas so the atlas + indirection MATCH VT3's --vt-pagegen) and dispatches shaders/vt_sample.comp — ONE thread per VIRTUAL texel (a race-free parallel MAP, NO atomics) — reconstructing the mip-0 virtual image (16 pages/side × 64-px page = 1024×1024): each virtual texel runs SampleVirtualTexel (copied VERBATIM from render/vt.h) -> its pageId -> gIndirection[pageId] -> the physical tile -> the atlas texel; non-resident page -> kVtMiss. ReadBuffer reads the RGBA8-packed virtual image and it is proven BIT-EXACT vs the CPU vt::ReconstructVirtualImage reference (memcmp, NO FP tolerance — the GPU sample reproduces the CPU image); round-trip self-consistency (every resident virtual texel == PageTexel(pageId,mip,lx,ly) — the sample read back exactly what VT3 wrote — every non-resident == kVtMiss); page-lookup @interior GPU==CPU bit-exact; sampleEnabled=false -> all kVtMiss (the disabled no-op); two dispatches byte-identical. The image golden is the reconstructed virtual image decoded directly from the RGBA8-packed uints (resident pages textured at their virtual-UV location, non-resident pages the magenta miss color — visually DISTINCT from VT3's physical-atlas tile-alloc order) -> identical both backends by construction; reuses BufferUsage::Storage + DispatchCompute + ReadBuffer — no new RHI symbol).

Runtime Virtual Texturing per-page CACHING across frames (Slice VT5, the 5th and FINAL Runtime Virtual Texturing slice — completing the RVT core after VT1's feedback + VT2's allocation + VT3's page-gen + VT4's sample; the direct analog of VSM Slice VD's PageContentKey/VsmPageCache. VT3 regenerates EVERY allocated page each frame; VT5 SKIPS regenerating physical tiles whose page CONTENT is unchanged across frames — the key perf property of a runtime virtual texture (generate/stream a page once, reuse it until its source changes). A page's content is FULLY determined by (pageId, mip, contentVersion) since the generator PageTexelV is deterministic, so a per-page FNV content key vt::PageContentKey(pageId,mip,contentVersion) captures 'has this page's content changed'; a per-physical-tile vt::VtPageCache (last key + valid bit) decides per tile: vt::PageCacheHit -> SKIP (the tile's prior atlas texels persist byte-identical), else regenerate via PageTexelV (with contentVersion folded into the seed; version 0 reproduces VT3's PageTexel exactly so the VT3/VT4 goldens are unchanged). The host compares keys and builds a per-tile needsGen flag array; shaders/vt_cachegen.comp (a cache-aware sibling of VT3's UNCHANGED vt_pagegen.comp) is ONE thread per atlas texel over a PERSISTENT atlas SSBO (NOT re-cleared) — gNeedsGen[tile]==0 (cached or unallocated) RETURNS without writing so the texel keeps its prior value, else writes PageTexelV(pageId,mip,localXY,gTileVersion[tile]). Reuses VT3/VT4's scene (the SAME VtTexture mipLevels=4/pageSize=128/vpps0=16 + 576 (UV,mip) requests -> host vt::MarkFeedbackPages -> vt::AllocatePhysicalTiles over VtTilePool{tilesPerSide=16} -> vt::BuildTilePageId), then runs three passes over the persistent atlas: Pass1 populate (empty cache -> all allocated tiles needsGen=1), Pass2 all-cached (same versions -> all PageCacheHit -> needsGen=0, the dispatch writes nothing), Pass3 invalidate one (bump ONE allocated page's contentVersion 0->1 -> only that tile needsGen=1). PROVEN (fail-loud): all-cached==fresh BYTE-IDENTICAL with misses2==0 (the cache is a pure optimization — a hit skips work that would produce identical bytes); invalidation minimal (misses3==1) AND transparent (the partial cached regen == a full uncached regen of the same version-state, BYTE-IDENTICAL); the Pass3 GPU atlas == the CPU vt::GenerateCachedAtlas reference BIT-EXACT (memcmp, NO FP); the 3-pass sequence run twice -> identical final atlas. The image golden is a cache-status viz of the Pass3 state — each allocated tile shows its content tinted GREEN for cached / RED for the 1 regenerated tile, dark for unallocated (the vsm_cache viz lineage) -> identical both backends by construction; reuses BufferUsage::Storage + DispatchCompute + ReadBuffer — no new RHI symbol).
Multithreaded command recording (per-thread secondaries, 1-vs-N identical).
Docked editor UI (Scene Hierarchy / Inspector / Stats panels around a central scene Viewport, fixed selected entity).
Editor live-edit: apply a fixed transform+material edit to the selected entity, render the EDITED docked scene, round-trip through scene_io (DumpScene/LoadScene).
Editor selection + translate gizmo overlay.
Scripted-pose interactive-runtime capture.
Deterministic audio mixer (integer/fixed-point voices -> 16-bit PCM WAV).
